dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7359 IH: Somalia Immigration Moratorium Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Gill of Texas (for himself, Mrs. Miller of Illinois, Mr. Fine, Mr. Biggs of Arizona, and Mr. Higgins of Louisiana) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend section 212 of the Immigration and Nationality Act to prohibit immigration relief for certain citizens or nationals of Somalia.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Somalia Immigration Moratorium Act.

paragraph.text: It is the purpose of the United States Government to guarantee survival and safety of the United States as an independent, sovereign republic whose government secures the God-given natural rights of its citizens and prioritizes their wellbeing and interests.
The United States Government reserves the sovereign right to admit or deny entry into the United States any foreign person.

subsection.text: The purpose of this Act is to establish a moratorium on immigration concerning citizens and nationals of Somalia.

section.header: Prohibition on immigration relief for certain citizens or nationals of Somalia

subsection.text: Section 212(a) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1182(a)) is amended by inserting at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, except as provided in subparagraph (B), no alien may be issued a visa or provided any status under the immigration laws, until 25 years following the date of enactment of the Somalia Immigration Moratorium Act.
Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to the following:

clause.text: Any alien lawfully admitted to the United States prior to the date of enactment of Somalia Immigration Moratorium Act.
Any lawful permanent resident of the United States.
Any alien traveling with valid nonimmigrant visa in the following classifications: A–1, A–2, C–2, C–3, G–1, G–2, G–3, or G–4.

subparagraph.continuation-text: These exceptions shall be made by only the Secretary of State or his or her designee, in coordination with the Secretary of Homeland Security or his or her designee.

subsection.text: Section 241(b)(3) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1231(b)(3)) is amended by inserting at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: Any alien who is a citizen or national of Somalia.

section.text: If any provision of this Act, an amendment by this Act, or the application of such provision or amendment to any person or circumstance is held to be unconstitutional, the remainder of this Act, the amendments made by this Act, and the application of such provision or amendment to any person or circumstance shall not be affected thereby.
